meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your SaaS projects. Master idea validation using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your next build generates real revenue.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is building without market validation. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you pivot from 'coding for fun' to 'building for profit.' Validate early, ship a lean MVP, and confirm customer willingness to pay before writing complex backend logic.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single paying user. You start with a burst of adrenaline, architecting elegant systems, only to hit the wall of feature creep and market silence. The project stalls, your motivation craters, and another promising idea dies in the repository.

But what if the problem isn't your coding ability, but your sequence of operations? By shifting your focus from 'building' to 'validating,' you transform your development process from a guessing game into a revenue-generating engine. We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to strip away the vanity metrics and build exactly what your market is begging for.

steps.title: Define the Value Prop Hypothesis
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific pain point and the 'willingness to pay' metric. Without this, you are just writing expensive hobbies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Review this business idea [INSERT IDEA] and generate a 3-point value proposition hypothesis focusing on high-intent user problems.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze this problem statement and identify the top 3 risky assumptions that could kill this product.'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. Don't waste time on auth or UI styling; focus on the core utility that solves the user's main problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a minimal SaaS stack using Next.js and Supabase. Include only the essential feature: [INSERT FEATURE]. Exclude all non-critical components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Initialize project structure with strictly minimal dependencies for MVP deployment.'

steps.title: Build the Core Utility
steps.description: Implement the one feature that provides immediate value. If the user can't experience the 'aha' moment in under 60 seconds, your code is too complex.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic for the core utility [INSERT UTILITY]. Ensure the code is modular, testable, and focused on speed of execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the core logic for [FEATURE] with robust error handling and clear logs.'

steps.title: Automated Feedback Loop
steps.description: Integrate a simple feedback trigger. You need to know if the user found the solution helpful the moment they use it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lightweight feedback component that captures user sentiment after they use the core utility. Store results in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Add a feedback capture component that triggers after the core function executes.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push to production immediately. The goal is to get 10 real users to interact with the tool within 24 hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare a deployment checklist for this MVP. Identify any potential bottlenecks in the current code that would prevent a fast production launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review codebase for production readiness and suggest the fastest deployment path to Vercel.'

pro_tips: Never build a dashboard until you have at least 5 paying customers; focus exclusively on the 'utility' view.
If you find yourself writing more than 100 lines of code without testing the concept, stop and talk to a potential user.
Use Claude Code to refactor for simplicity, not performance. Premature optimization is the primary killer of MVP momentum.
Prioritize 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) over feature depth; users pay for results, not for a massive feature list.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to build, break it down into smaller micro-interactions or reconsider its necessity.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, shift your focus to customer acquisition. Use the feedback gathered in Step 4 to iterate on the core value proposition, not to add new features.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop stalling and start shipping. Don't let your next great idea become another abandoned repo. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
